name: Ascent to Pico Mágina and Miramundos
name_original: Pico Mágina - circular trailrunning por el parque natural de Sierra Mágina
description: Challenging 13.7-mile trail run to Pico Mágina and Miramundos with 3653 feet of gain, offering vast views in Sierra Mágina.
region_intro: Jogging around Cambil offers diverse terrain within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, characterized by a mountainous backdrop and varied inclines. The region features rivers like the Villanueva, Oviedo, and Cambil, which carve out valleys, alongside extensive olive groves and agricultural landscapes. Geological formations, including dramatic rock walls and gorges, contribute to the scenic environment for running. Best jogging routes around Cambil The most popular jogging route is Ascent to Pico Mágina and Miramundos, a 13.7 miles (22.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 20 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain and panoramic views of the Sierra Mágina Natural Park. Another top favourite among local runners is Pico Mágina Summit (2,167 m) – Miramundos Shelter loop from Cortijo de Vista Alegre, a difficult 12.4 miles (20.0 km) path. This challenging trail leads to the summit of Pico Mágina, providing expansive mountain vistas. Local runners also love the Miramundos Shelter loop from Cortijo de Vista Alegre, a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail leading through the high mountain terrain of Sierra Mágina, often completed in about 2 hours 39 minutes. Jogging around Cambil is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and olive groves. The network of routes offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Cambil are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Cambil's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running trails are available in Cambil?
A: There are over 20 running routes around Cambil, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and fitness levels. The area is particularly known for its diverse terrain within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of running routes in Cambil?
A: The running routes in Cambil offer a variety of difficulty levels. While there are 14 difficult trails and 9 moderate options, there is also 1 easy route available, ensuring options for runners of all abilities. The mountainous terrain of Sierra Mágina Natural Park often means significant elevation changes.

Q: What do other runners think about the trails in Cambil?
A: The running routes in Cambil are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Cambil's varied terrain, often praising the challenging mountain vistas and the natural beauty of the Sierra Mágina Natural Park.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Cambil?
A: Yes, while many routes in Cambil are challenging, there is at least one easy running route available. The region also features 'Rutas Urbanas' and a 'Ruta Saludable' like 'La Torrecilla' within and around the town, which are generally well-maintained and suitable for less strenuous jogs or beginners.

Q: Can I find long-distance running trails in Cambil?
A: Absolutely. Cambil offers several long-distance running trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989314133/ascent-to-pico-magina-and-miramundos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Pico Mágina and Miramundos&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 13.7-mile (22.1 km) route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mágina Summit (2,167 m) – Miramundos Shelter loop from Cortijo de Vista Alegre&lt;/a&gt; covers 12.4 miles (20.0 km), both offering extensive mountain running experiences.

Q: Are there running routes in Cambil with scenic viewpoints?
A: Yes, Cambil is rich in scenic viewpoints, especially within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989314133/ascent-to-pico-magina-and-miramundos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Pico Mágina and Miramundos&lt;/a&gt; offer panoramic views. The region's particular orography, with the town situated between dramatic rock walls and features like the Puerta Arenas Gorge, ensures breathtaking vistas along many trails.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the running routes in Cambil?
A: Cambil's running routes often pass through stunning natural landscapes. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuadros River&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Agua de Bedmar&lt;/a&gt;, or reach the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4444835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mágina Summit (2,167 m)&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adelfal de Cuadros Trail&lt;/a&gt; is another beautiful natural highlight.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks visible from the jogging paths?
A: Yes, the region's rich history means you can often spot historical landmarks. The castles of Cambil and Alhabar, perched on rocky outcrops, are prominent features that add a cultural dimension to your run. The historical urban core of Cambil itself is recognized as Andalusian Historical Heritage.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Cambil?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Cambil are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40305992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miramundos Shelter loop from Cortijo de Vista Alegre&lt;/a&gt; is a 10.6-mile (17.1 km) circular trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Torres&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a 13.1 km circular experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when running in Cambil?
A: You can expect highly varied terrain. Cambil is characterized by mountainous landscapes within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, featuring significant inclines and descents. You'll also find routes alongside rivers like the Villanueva, Oviedo, and Cambil, which often offer flatter sections, as well as extensive olive groves and agricultural fields providing a different sensory experience.

Q: Are there running routes that pass by water features?
A: Yes, Cambil is irrigated by the Villanueva, Oviedo, and Cambil rivers, and features numerous springs and ditches. Many routes follow these waterways, offering pleasant, often flatter, sections alongside them. The presence of water bodies contributes to lush vegetation and a soothing auditory backdrop for your run.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Cambil?
A: Cambil offers good jogging conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for outdoor activities. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, but higher mountain routes in Sierra Mágina Natural Park may offer cooler conditions. Winters are mild, making it possible to run year-round, though some higher trails might be affected by snow.

Q: Are there any running routes suitable for families with children?
A: While many routes in Cambil are challenging, the municipality also offers 'Rutas Urbanas' and a 'Ruta Saludable' like 'La Torrecilla' that are generally well-maintained and suitable for families or those looking for an easier pace. These paths often provide a gentler introduction to the region's natural beauty.
tips: Summit located in the homonymous natural park in the province of Jaén. With its 2,167 m altitude, it is the highest peak in this province and is located on the dividing line between the municipalities of Albanchez de Mágina and Huelma.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ascent to Pico Mágina and Miramundos trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and challenging. Approximately 50% consists of dirt tracks, while the other 50% is a mountain path, largely characterized by rocky surfaces and limestone slabs. You'll encounter steep ascents with inclinations up to 21% and even 26% in some sections. The path can be rugged and stony, especially in the high mountain areas.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for jogging or beginners?
faqs.answer: While listed as a jogging route, this trail is considered to have high difficulty and is very demanding. The significant elevation gain and rocky, steep surfaces make continuous jogging difficult. It's primarily suitable for experienced hikers and trail runners looking for an intense workout, not for beginners or casual jogging.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular panoramic views. Key highlights include the Pico Mágina Summit (2,167 m), which is the highest point in Jaén province, and the Miramundos Refuge (2,077 m). From these points, you can often see Sierra Nevada, Sierra Arana, Sierra de Cazorla, and the vast olive groves of Jaén. You'll also pass by historical elements like ancient snow wells and shepherd's huts.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to tackle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to tackle this route is generally outside of winter. During winter, the trail can be covered in snow and ice, and you may encounter low temperatures, strong winds, and increased hazards, making it significantly more difficult and potentially dangerous. Spring and autumn offer more favorable conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to access the Sierra Mágina Natural Park or this trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Sierra Mágina Natural Park or this particular trail. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations before your visit, especially regarding protected areas and potential restrictions during certain seasons (e.g., fire risk).

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific trailhead parking information is not detailed, but access points to trails in the Sierra Mágina Natural Park typically involve parking near villages or designated recreational areas. It's recommended to research the most common starting points for Pico Mágina ascents to find suitable parking options.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on the trail?
faqs.answer: While many natural parks in Spain allow dogs, they are typically required to be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other users. Always check the specific regulations for the Parque Natural Sierra Mágina regarding pets before you go.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this route?
faqs.answer: This route is approximately 22 km long with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain. For an experienced trail runner, it takes around 3 hours and 20 minutes. However, given its challenging nature and significant elevation, hikers should expect a considerably longer duration, likely 6-8 hours or more, depending on pace and stops.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the Sierra Mágina Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notable sections include parts of the "Un paseo por el Karst de Sierra Mágina" and "Vereda de Mágina" trails, among others like "Andalucía - North Route (Província de Jaén)" and "Puerto de la Mata".

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plants might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a pure high-mountain environment with unique flora and fauna. You might spot endemic plants like Viola cazorlensis and Lithodora nitida. Wildlife sightings can include mountain goats and golden eagles, especially in the higher elevations.

faqs.question: What should I know about tackling this route in winter?
faqs.answer: Attempting this route in winter requires significant preparation and experience. The trail can be covered in snow and ice, and you should be prepared for low temperatures, strong winds, and potentially hazardous conditions. Proper winter gear, including crampons and an ice axe, may be necessary, and it's crucial to check weather forecasts before heading out.
